cluster_context:
AI-enhanced document editing and processing tools in pdfFiller's browser-based 
editor. Frames pdfFiller's core capabilities as AI-powered or AI-assisted 
across four functional groups:

AI PDF editing: edit, modify, format, replace text, rewrite, analyze — 
applied to PDF, DOC, DOCX, RTF, and other document types
AI signature: add, insert, create, draw, type, write — all signature 
action variants (e-signature, digital signature, online signature)
AI form filling: fill PDF forms and complete documents with AI-powered 
assistance
AI conversion: convert between PDF, Word, Excel, PPT, PPTX, and image 
formats using AI-enhanced tools

Key operations: edit, sign, esign, fill, create, add, extract, generate, 
rewrite, convert, format, analyze, modify.

Accuracy rules:
- AI-enhanced tools perform the same underlying actions as standard 
  pdfFiller tools — the AI framing accelerates and guides the workflow, 
  it does not replace user review
- AI rewriter modifies existing document text — it does not generate 
  documents from scratch; document drafting from scratch is a separate 
  feature (Create Document with AI)
- AI form filler assists with field completion — it does not place 
  fillable fields; field creation is a separate Add Fields workflow
- Do not promise AI performs autonomous editing without user review 
  or guarantee output accuracy
- Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat AI Assistant, Smallpdf AI, 
  DocuSign AI, Foxit AI — differentiate on pdfFiller's browser-based 
  AI covering editing + signing + converting + form filling in one workflow

cluster_context:
PDF annotation and markup tools in pdfFiller's browser-based editor, activated via the Edit Mode toggle.
 

 Tools menu: Highlight, Draw, Line, Arrow, Sticky Note, Blackout, Watermark, Date Stamp, Page Numbering, Bates Numbering
 Main toolbar: Circle, Checkmark, Cross, Text Box, Erase
 

 Functional groupings:
 Markup: Highlight, Draw, Line, Arrow — visual emphasis layers
 Symbols: Checkmark, Cross, Circle — one-click placement on scanned forms
 Text: Text Box — static free-floating annotation (not a fillable field)
 Redaction: Blackout — permanently hides content on finalization
 Document-wide: Watermark, Date Stamp, Page Numbering, Bates Numbering
 Remove: Erase — removes placed annotations only, not original content
 

 All tools overlay the document visually; underlying content is never altered. All tools work on both native and scanned PDFs.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - Text Box is a static annotation, not a fillable field
 - Erase removes annotations only, not original document content
 - Sticky Note does not print by default
 - Blackout is permanent and unrecoverable after download
 - Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, Smallpdf, PDF Expert, Foxit

cluster_context:
Branding tools in pdfFiller let users embed visual brand marks into documents and configure account-level email branding. Two distinct layers are covered:
 

 Document-level branding:
 Image Tool (logo placement): open document → toolbar → Image icon → upload image → position and resize
 Watermark Tool: open document → Tools → Watermark → choose Text or Image watermark → configure size, transparency, rotation, page scope → Apply
 

 Workspace-level branding:
 Custom email signature / footer: Account Settings → Custom Branding → configure email signature or footer fields
 

 Operations covered: adding, placing, editing, replacing, and removing logo or trademark images on PDFs and other document types; applying and removing image or text watermarks; creating a custom logo at account level; creating a custom email signature; creating a custom email footer; branding document templates.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - "Trademark" in this cluster means visual brand mark (logo image placed on a document), not legal IP registration
 - Watermark Tool applies repeating or full-page watermark overlays; Image Tool places a discrete logo element
 - Custom email branding (signature, footer) is workspace-level configuration, not per-document
 - Do not conflate with password protection (Protect) or text editing (Edit)
 - Named competitors are not applicable for this cluster

cluster_context:
PDF file size optimization tool in pdfFiller's browser-based editor.
 

 Access paths:
 Editor header: Compress button in top toolbar (requires active editing session)
 All Tools dashboard: accessible without an open document
 

 Mechanism: optimizes embedded images, flattens annotation and form field layers, applies file-level compression. Page content, text, structure, annotations, and form field data remain intact.
 

 Quality levels (plan-dependent):
 High — minimal visual change, modest size reduction
 Medium — balanced size/quality tradeoff
 Low — maximum size reduction, most visible image quality impact
 

 Yield varies by content: image-heavy and scanned PDFs compress significantly; text-only PDFs yield minimal reduction; already-optimized files yield near-zero reduction.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - Never promise specific size reduction percentages
 - Quality loss applies to images only, not text or form data
 - Compress, Delete Pages, Split, and Save As solve different problems
 - Quality level selection is plan-tier dependent
 - Named competitors: Smallpdf, ilovepdf, Adobe Acrobat, Foxit

cluster_context:
PDF format conversion tool in pdfFiller.
 

 Access paths:
 Done dropdown (editor): Done → Save As → choose output format
 All Tools (dashboard): Convert to Word / Excel / PowerPoint / Images card
 More menu (document row): document-level conversion without entering the editor
 

 Supported conversions:
 PDF → Word (.docx), Excel (.xlsx), PowerPoint (.pptx), Image (.jpg / .png)
 Non-PDF → PDF: Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and image files convert automatically on upload
 

 Mechanism: server-side conversion, downloads immediately after processing. No software installation required.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - Native PDFs convert with higher fidelity; scanned PDFs produce image-based output in Word unless OCR is applied
 - Layout fidelity is not guaranteed for complex formatting (columns, tables, graphics)
 - Image conversion produces one .jpg/.png per page, not a single image for multi-page documents
 - Plan-tier restrictions may apply
 - Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, ilovepdf, Smallpdf, Nitro, Foxit

cluster_context:
Document template creation in pdfFiller — building a reusable fillable document structure and saving it for repeated use.
 

 Two creation paths:
 Upload new file: upload a document → open in editor → use Add Fields mode to add fillable fields → save as template
 From existing document: My Docs → More menu → Tools → Convert to Template → choose Use As Is or Add Fields → save
 

 Templates are saved to Personal Templates (creator only) or Team Templates (shared across workspace). The original template is preserved on each reuse — every completion saves as a new document, not over the template.
 

 Reuse workflow: open template → parties fill their fields → complete, sign, and save or send. Templates can also be distributed as a fill link via LinkToFill.
 

 Add Fields mode is the core: text fields, checkboxes, dropdowns, signature fields, date fields, initials, and required-field designation.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - A template is a fillable document structure — "create" means building the fillable form layer, not authoring legal text
 - Personal vs. Team templates have different access scope
 - LinkToFill distributes a template as a fill link — distinct from sending a PDF attachment
 - Named competitors: DocuSign, JotForm, PandaDoc, Adobe Acrobat Sign

cluster_context:
Crop and resize tools in pdfFiller's web editor let users change the dimensions or visible area of PDF pages. Two distinct operations:
 

 Crop: removes unwanted content or margins by defining a crop box — a selection frame whose boundaries determine which portion of the page is kept.
 Resize: changes the overall page canvas dimensions to a preset standard (A4, Letter) or custom width and height.
 Make PDF Pages Same Size: normalizes all pages in a multi-page document to uniform dimensions.
 

 Access paths:
 Crop tool: open document in editor → toolbar → Crop → drag crop box edges/corners → Apply
 Resize tool: open document in editor → toolbar → Resize → select preset or enter custom dimensions → Apply
 Make Pages Same Size: open document in editor → Page tab → Make PDF Pages Same Size
 

 Operations covered: cropping a PDF page; cropping using the crop box; resizing a PDF; resizing to A4 or Letter; changing PDF page size; making all pages the same size; resizing to custom dimensions; crop and edit compound workflows; all the above on specific devices and browsers.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - Crop adjusts visible content area; Resize changes page canvas dimensions — distinguish clearly
 - "Resize PDF" for file size reduction belongs to Compress, not this cluster
 - Make Pages Same Size is page-normalization, not compression or conversion
 - Page reordering, rotation, and deletion belong to Rearrange Pages, not this cluster
 - Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, Smallpdf, iLovePDF

cluster_context:
PDF text editing tools in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Accessed via the Edit button (pencil icon) in the editor toolbar, which activates Edit Mode. Covers direct modification of document content — editing, adding, and removing text — and text formatting.
 

 Core capabilities:
 Edit existing text — click directly on text blocks in native PDFs to modify content
 Add text — insert new free-floating text blocks anywhere on the document
 Delete/remove text — select and delete existing text content
 Whiteout — covers underlying content with a white fill rectangle for correction purposes
 Text formatting: font family, size, bold, italic, underline, text color, alignment
 Find & Replace — locate and replace specific text strings across the document
 

 Native vs. scanned PDFs:
 Native PDFs: full text editing with best-effort font matching
 Scanned PDFs: text is image-based — direct editing unavailable without OCR; use annotation tools to overlay corrections
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - Edit Mode modifies content directly; annotation tools overlay without modifying
 - Text editing on scanned PDFs requires OCR first
 - Whiteout ≠ Blackout — Whiteout is a correction tool; Blackout is permanent redaction
 - Font matching is best-effort, not guaranteed
 - Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, Foxit, Nitro, Sejda

cluster_context:
pdfFiller's embed cluster covers three distinct embedding concepts:
 

 1. Native in-document embedding: pdfFiller's editor lets users embed files (images, documents) and videos directly into a PDF as elements within the PDF structure.
 

 2. Embedding a PDF in external environments: pdfFiller generates an iframe embed code to display a PDF on a website; for embedding in Word or Excel, pdfFiller guides users through those applications' native Object insertion workflows.
 

 3. pdfFiller embedded editor SDK: a developer-facing product at embed.pdffiller.com that lets software companies integrate the full pdfFiller editor into their own web applications via iframe or SDK.
 

 Access paths:
 Embed file in PDF: editor → toolbar → Embed File / Attach → select file
 Embed video in PDF: editor → toolbar → Add Video → upload or paste URL
 Embed PDF in HTML: document → Share → Embed → copy iframe code
 Insert PDF into Google Docs: use pdfFiller Google Docs add-on or convert PDF to Google Doc
 Embedded editor SDK: embed.pdffiller.com (developer product)
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - "Embed PDF in Word" and "Attach PDF in Excel" use Word's/Excel's native Object insertion — pdfFiller facilitates workflow, not the embedding step itself
 - "Embedded editor" pages refer to the developer SDK at embed.pdffiller.com
 - Embed video is a native editor feature; distinct from embedding a PDF on a website
 - Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, DocHub

pdfFiller's online fax service lets users send and receive faxes directly from their account without a physical fax machine. Users can fax any document in their library to a recipient's fax number, optionally selecting specific pages and adding a cover page.  Receive fax: Received → Faxes → Get fax number if not yet assigned
 

 Operations covered: sending a fax online; faxing a PDF to a fax number; faxing via email/Gmail; one-time fax (no subscription); receiving a fax; getting a personal fax number; HIPAA-compliant faxing; faxing for organizations; fax PDF on specific devices; comparing pdfFiller fax to competitors.

 - "Send fax via email" means routing to a fax number via email-to-fax — not simply emailing a document
 - Receiving faxes requires obtaining a personal pdfFiller fax number
 - Named competitors: HelloFax, eFax, MyFax, Smallpdf, RingCentral Fax

Fillable field creation and management in pdfFiller's browser-based editor, accessed via the Add Fields toggle in the editor top bar. The Add Fillable Fields panel (left side) contains all field types as draggable items; the properties panel (right side) handles per-field configuration.
 

 Purpose: convert any static PDF or uploaded document into an interactive form so recipients can type, select, check, or sign without altering the document layout.
 

 Full field library:
 Standard: Text, Number, Date, Time, Image
 Identity/structured: Name, Company, Title, Email, US Phone, ZIP, SSN, EIN, Age, US States, Gender
 Selection: Checkbox, Radio Button (mutually exclusive groups), Dropdown
 Signature: Signature, Initials
 Logic: Formula/Calculated Field
 Currency: USD, EUR
 

 All fields can be resized, repositioned, renamed, marked required, and styled. Fields integrate with the Recipients panel for multi-signer assignment.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - Annotation Checkmark ≠ Add Fields Checkbox; Text Box annotation ≠ Text Field
 - Formula fields operate within the same document only
 - Currency fields apply formatting only — no conversion
 - Radio buttons are mutually exclusive; checkboxes are independent
 - Add Fields works on scanned PDFs without OCR
 - Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, JotForm, DocuSign, Foxit, PDF Expert

pdfFiller's platform integrations connect pdfFiller's PDF editing, filling, and signing tools to external cloud storage and business platforms, allowing users to open, fill, edit, sign, and save documents without leaving the connected service.
 

 Primary integrations:
 Google Drive (add-on from Google Workspace Marketplace), Dropbox (native integration), OneDrive (native integration), Box (native integration), Xero (native integration), Salesforce (via airSlate workflow automation), SharePoint (Microsoft AppSource add-in), Slack (via Zapier/Make automation), Office 365 / Microsoft 365 (Office Add-in), Google Docs (Google Workspace add-on).
 

 General workflow: connect the external account to pdfFiller → open files directly from that service → fill, edit, annotate, or sign using pdfFiller's editor → save changes back to the original service.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - This cluster covers the integration connection and workflow — not the pdfFiller features themselves
 - Slack integration is automation-based (Zapier/Make), not a direct file-opening integration
 - Salesforce integration connects via airSlate's workflow automation platform — describe by workflow capability, not by a specific product name
 - "Google Drive docs" and "Google Docs" are distinct: Google Drive is storage; Google Docs is the document-creation app
 - Do not conflate the Office 365 add-in with the OneDrive integration
 - Named competitors are not explicitly present in this cluster's corpus

pdfFiller's HIPAA compliance mode lets Business plan customers configure their account to meet HIPAA requirements for handling electronic Protected Health Information (ePHI). Enabled via Account Settings → Authentication and Access Security → HIPAA compliance toggle, after which the customer signs a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) with pdfFiller.
 

 When HIPAA mode is active, pdfFiller applies SHA-256 encryption for all data transfers and document storage, stores documents in HIPAA-compliant US-based Amazon S3 data centers, and enables strict access controls, audit trail logging, and anti-tampering protections.
 

 Important constraint: enabling HIPAA compliance disables Fax, Notarize, and Send via USPS delivery channels.
 

 Access paths:
 Enable HIPAA: click account icon → My Account → Settings → Authentication and Access Security → HIPAA compliance toggle → contact support to sign BAA
 Audit trail: Account → Audit Trail
 Access controls: document-level permissions, encrypted folders, two-factor authentication, document passwords
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - HIPAA compliance is a Business plan account-level setting — applies to the entire account once enabled
 - When HIPAA mode is enabled, Fax, Notarize, and Send via USPS are disabled
 - "HIPAA-compliant electronic signature" is the standard pdfFiller e-signature operating under HIPAA-mode security — not a separate product
 - Do not make specific legal or regulatory compliance guarantees beyond what pdfFiller's own documentation states
 - Named competitors: SodaPDF

cluster_context:
Image manipulation tool in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Accessed via the Image icon in the main toolbar — clicking it opens a file selection window; the selected image is placed on the active page as a movable, resizable overlay element.
 

 Supported input formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF (BMP in some environments).
 

 Operations after placement:
 Resize: drag corner handles; aspect ratio preserved by default
 Reposition: drag image to any location on the page
 Rotate: freely rotate the image element
 Layer order: bring forward / send backward relative to other elements
 Opacity: adjust image transparency (plan-dependent)
 Duplicate / Delete: copy or remove the image element
 

 Crop/cut-out: no dedicated dialog — resize the element to expose the desired area, or delete and re-upload a pre-cropped image. "Merge photo" and "edit photo" keyword variants map to the same workflow: upload → place → resize/reposition.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - No separate crop, cut-out, or replace image tool
 - Opacity control is plan-dependent
 - Image Tool adds images as overlay elements, not embedded into the PDF content layer
 - Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF — not SVG, TIFF, or WebP
 - Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, PDF24, ilovepdf, Smallpdf

cluster_context:
Remote online notarization (RON) service in pdfFiller — a workflow that sends a document to pdfFiller's third-party notarization partner for a live video session with a commissioned online notary. pdfFiller acts as the submission interface; the notarization is performed by an independent partner service.
 

 Access path:
 Document row menu → Send → Notarize
 Sent → Notarize subfolder (tracks submitted and completed requests)
 All Tools panel on the dashboard (Notarize card)
 

 Workflow:
 1. Select document → Send → Notarize
 2. Confirm document and enter signer details (name, country; option to add additional signers)
 3. Identity verification (government-issued ID required)
 4. Live video call with a commissioned online notary
 5. Notarized document returned to Sent → Notarize folder
 

 Geographic coverage: RON is legally available in most but not all US states; rules on document types vary by state. State-specific pages live under /notarization/[state].
 

 Common document types: bill of sale, lease agreement, affidavit, quitclaim deed, power of attorney, warranty deed, employment verification letter, contract.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - pdfFiller is the submission interface; the notarization is performed by a third-party partner
 - Government-issued ID is required during the video session
 - RON availability varies by US state — do not claim universal availability
 - Notarization ≠ e-signature, witnessed signature, or document certification
 - Named competitors: Notarize by Proof, NotaryCam, DocVerify, OneNotary

cluster_context:
PDF password security features in pdfFiller. The primary tool is Protect Document (Tools → Protect Document): users select a document and page scope — entire document or specific pages — enter a password, and click Apply. The protected PDF requires that password to open, whether downloaded locally or shared via a pdfFiller link.
 

 A secondary security feature is the Confidential folder in My Documents. It requires phone verification and a custom password to access, and hides documents from Recent and main document lists. This is workspace-level access control inside pdfFiller — not a PDF file-level password.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - Protect Document adds an open-entry password; it does not prevent printing or screenshots
 - The Confidential folder is workspace security (in-app), not file-level PDF encryption
 - "Unlock PDF" means opening a file the user already knows the password for — not a password bypass tool
 - "Make PDF read-only" is accomplished via Protect Document
 - Access path is Tools → Protect Document
 - Named competitors: ilovepdf, Smallpdf, pdf2go, Adobe Acrobat

cluster_context:
Page management tools in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Covers all structural page operations — reordering, rotating, deleting, inserting, and duplicating — without creating separate files.
 

 Access paths:
 Dashboard: Rearrange quick-action card
 Document context: Tools → Rearrange pages (More menu in MyDocs)
 Inside editor: Manage button (under page thumbnails) → Manage modal for batch operations
 Inside editor: Sidebar thumbnail controls for quick single-page actions
 

 Operations:
 Reorder: drag-and-drop grid, Move left/right, Move to (jump to specific page), multi-select
 Rotate: 90° clockwise per click; single or multiple pages; preview before Apply
 Delete: sidebar trash icon (single page); Manage modal batch delete; confirmation dialog if page contains form fields
 Add blank page: sidebar "+" buttons, sidebar bottom button, Manage modal "Add page"
 Duplicate: copies all page content including annotations and form fields
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - All operations modify the existing document — they do not create separate files (that is Split's function)
 - Rotate increments at 90° clockwise only
 - Deleting a page with form fields triggers a confirmation dialog
 - Add Blank Page inserts an empty editable page — not a page from another document
 - Named competitors: ilovepdf, Smallpdf, Adobe Acrobat, PDF24, Sejda

cluster_context:
E-signature request workflow in pdfFiller. Sends a document to one or multiple named recipients for legally binding electronic signing via a secure email link — recipients do not need a pdfFiller account.
 

 Access paths:
 Dashboard: "Request e-signatures" quick-action card
 MyDocs: document action menu → Request e-signatures
 Inside editor: Done dropdown → E-sign
 Inside editor (Add fields mode): Request E-Signatures panel → Review and Send
 

 Workflow: Open document → Add fields mode → add recipients by email with roles (Signer or Viewer) → assign signature and form fields to each recipient → configure signing order (sequential or parallel) → set reminders and expiration (optional) → click Review and Send → each signer receives a secure email link → track progress from Sent → E-signature requests.
 

 Key capabilities: multi-signer support, sequential or parallel signing order, automated reminders, expiration deadlines, audit trail, signed PDF auto-saved.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - Recipients do not need a pdfFiller account
 - "Send to sign" and "Request e-signatures" are the same workflow
 - Signing order is configurable (sequential or parallel)
 - Audit trail availability may be plan-dependent
 - Do not conflate with the Sign tool (self-signing) or LinkToFill (mass anonymous collection)
 - Named competitors: DocuSign, Adobe Acrobat Sign, PandaDoc, SignWell, Dropbox Sign

cluster_context:
OCR (Optical Character Recognition) and scanned document processing tools in pdfFiller — converting image-based or scanned PDFs into editable, searchable, and extractable text, and handling image-to-PDF conversion and scan quality improvement.
 

 Access paths:
 OCR: upload document → OCR button in toolbar → language selection → Start OCR
 All Tools panel: OCR tool card accessible without opening a document
 Scan to PDF: upload image file (JPG, PNG, photo) → pdfFiller converts to PDF
 Clean Up Scanned PDF: filter panel — erase black borders, auto deskew, punch hole removal, despeckle
 Sharpen PDF: sharpening level selector (Low / Medium / High) + Enhance option
 Extract in Bulk: select template → Tools → Extract in Bulk → upload scanned files → download extracted data as spreadsheet
 

 Key capabilities: OCR engine with language selection, edit scanned PDF via two-step process (OCR then Edit Mode), scan image/JPG to PDF, Clean Up and Sharpen for image quality, DOCX OCR and non-PDF OCR support.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - "Edit scanned PDF" always requires: (1) apply OCR, (2) use Edit Mode
 - Scan to PDF does not apply OCR automatically
 - Clean Up and Sharpen improve visual fidelity but do not make text selectable
 - DOCX OCR and non-PDF OCR (Word, Excel, PPT, PNG) are valid paths
 - Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, Smallpdf, Sejda, ABBYY FineReader, Adobe Scan

cluster_context:
Sharing and delivery tools in pdfFiller let users distribute completed documents through multiple channels. Delivery methods include email, SMS/text message, fax, USPS physical mail, and shareable links. The Sent folder organizes all outgoing documents by channel (Email/SMS, Faxes, USPS mail, Notarize, E-signature requests, LinkToFill). Collaboration is supported via the Share for Collaboration workflow, which invites co-editors to work on a document.
 

 Access paths:
 Email/SMS: Done → Send via Email or SMS → enter recipient details
 Fax: Done → Send via Fax → enter fax number
 USPS mail: Done → Send via USPS mail → enter postal address
 Share for collaboration: My Docs → document row → Share → invite collaborators by email
 Sent folder: left sidebar → Sent → select subfolder by channel
 

 Operations covered: sending via email, SMS, or fax; mailing via USPS; sharing a PDF online via link; sharing for collaboration; emailing fillable forms; bulk sending; tracking sent documents.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - "Send and [action]" compound URLs belong here only when the delivery step is the primary topic
 - LinkToFill is a shareable fill-link feature — cover link generation and QR/embed sharing here
 - E-signature requests should be noted as a send channel but not described in depth
 - USPS mail is a physical mailing service — pdfFiller prints and mails on the user's behalf
 - Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat, DocuSign, Smallpdf

cluster_context:
Electronic signature tool in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Accessed via the Signature button in the main editor toolbar. Covers the self-sign flow — adding the user's own signature or initials — not requesting others' signatures.
 

 Three signing methods:
 Draw — freehand using mouse, touchpad, or stylus
 Type — styled text with selectable fonts and colors
 Upload — image file of an existing handwritten signature
 

 Initials: separate option with the same three input methods.
 

 Customization:
 Draw: color and line thickness
 Type: font style and color
 All: resize and reposition after placement
 Save & Reuse: signatures saved to account for reuse; user can set a default.
 

 Legal validity: compliant with ESIGN, UETA, eIDAS, and GDPR. Signed documents receive a timestamped audit trail and Verified by pdfFiller badge.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - Self-sign flow is separate from Send to Sign — no recipient requesting here
 - All three methods are equally legally binding
 - Saved signature is not auto-applied — user places it manually per document
 - Signature tool is not the same as Signature Field (fields for others are in Add Fields mode)
 - Named competitors: DocuSign, Adobe Acrobat Sign, Dropbox Sign, signNow

cluster_context:
PDF splitting and page extraction tool in pdfFiller's browser-based platform. Separates a multi-page PDF into smaller files without modifying the source document.
 

 Access paths:
 Dashboard: Split quick-action card (no document open required)
 Document context: Tools → Split (from the More menu in MyDocs)
 

 Three split modes:
 Extract pages: select specific individual pages to save as a new standalone PDF
 Split by range: define custom page ranges (e.g., pages 1–3, 4–7), each becoming a separate named output file
 Split by all pages: every page becomes its own individual PDF
 

 Additional capabilities: rename each output file before generating; output files saved to MyDocs; when many pages are split individually, output files are packaged as a ZIP archive.
 

 The source document remains unchanged after any split operation.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - "Extract pages" and "Split by range" are distinct modes within the same Split tool
 - Source PDF is not modified or deleted after splitting
 - When many pages are split individually, output is delivered as a ZIP package
 - Split and Delete Pages are distinct: Split creates new files; Delete Pages removes pages from the existing document
 - Named competitors: ilovepdf, Smallpdf, Adobe Acrobat Online, PDF24, Sejda

cluster_context:
pdfFiller provides native table tools in the PDF editor for inserting, editing, formatting, and extracting tables, and for generating a table of contents.
 

 Access paths:
 Insert table: editor → Insert tab → Table → select grid size → click to place
 Edit table: editor → click table cell → edit text; use table controls for rows, columns, resize, colors, borders
 Table of contents: editor → Insert tab → Page Templates → Table of contents → customize → Insert
 Extract tables: upload PDF → Extract Tables tool → download extracted data
 Fillable table: insert table → Add Fields mode → place fillable fields in table cells
 

 Key capabilities: insert table with custom grid, edit cells and structure, generate clickable auto-linked table of contents, extract tables from PDFs into editable formats (up to 10 files), place interactive fields inside table cells.
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - "Table of contents" and "data table" are distinct features — keep clearly separated
 - "Extract data from PDF to Excel" belongs here when the extraction target is table data; broader PDF-to-Excel format conversion belongs to Convert
 - Editing a "table in image/JPG" uses the Image Tool or OCR+edit approach, not a native table editor
 - Named competitors: Adobe Acrobat (table editing)

The table insert workflow: opening the Insert tab, selecting Table, choosing rows and columns, and placing the table.
## EDIT_TABLE — Editing a Table in a PDF
Table editing capabilities: changing cell text, adding/deleting rows and columns, resizing, changing colors and borders.
## FORMAT_TABLE — Formatting, Resizing, and Styling a Table
Formatting operations: resizing columns and rows, adjusting borders, applying background colors, sorting data.
## FILLABLE_TABLE — Creating a Fillable Table with Interactive Fields
How to make a table interactive by placing fillable fields inside cells via Add Fields mode.
## TABLE_OF_CONTENTS — Adding a Table of Contents to a PDF
The TOC feature: accessing via Insert → Page Templates, customizing styles, inserting the clickable TOC.
## EXTRACT_TABLES — Extracting Tables from a PDF
The Extract Tables feature: uploading a PDF, how pdfFiller detects table data, batch processing up to 10 files.
## EXTRACT_TO_EXCEL — Extracting PDF Table Data to Excel
How to pull structured table data from a PDF and export in Excel-compatible format.
## TABLE_OTHER_FORMATS — Table Operations in Images, JPG, Excel, PowerPoint, and WPS Files
Table operations when the source file is not a native PDF.
## DEVICE_BROWSER — Table Operations on Specific Devices and Browsers
Device- and browser-specific guidance for table operations.

3–4 professional scenarios where inserting, editing, or extracting a table in a PDF resolves a specific data presentation or extraction problem. Each scenario names a role from target_audience, a document type, and the specific table operation performed.
## FILE_FORMATS — Supported File Types for Table Operations
File types supported for table insertion and editing, and available export formats when extracting table data (including Excel, CSV, and plain text). Note format-specific behavior for scanned PDFs and image files.
## LIMITS — Limitations and Known Constraints
Table detection accuracy limitations on scanned PDFs, maximum table size for extraction, which table operations (fillable table fields, table of contents) require a paid plan, and supported export formats.
## SECURITY — Security and Data Handling
How uploaded documents are handled during and after this operation: TLS encryption in transit, file retention and automatic deletion timeline, and any compliance or audit considerations relevant to this workflow.

## FREE_ACCESS — Free vs. Paid Access for Table Tools
Which table operations are available on the free plan and which require a paid subscription. Reference plan tiers only — no prices.

cluster_context:
Watermark tool in pdfFiller's browser-based editor. Accessed via Tools → Watermark in the editor toolbar.
 

 Two watermark types:
 Text watermark: enter any text (e.g., "CONFIDENTIAL", "DRAFT", company name)
 Image watermark: upload a logo or emblem as an image overlay
 

 Customization: size, color, transparency/opacity, rotation (e.g., 45° diagonal or horizontal), page scope (current page, all pages, or custom page range).
 

 Edit or remove: reopen Tools → Watermark to modify or delete a previously applied watermark.
 

 The Watermark Panel also contains three document-wide stamping tools:
 Date Stamp: inserts today's date or a custom date on every page; configurable format, placement, font, transparency
 Page Numbering: adds sequential page numbers in formats like "1" or "Page 1 of 6"; placement and font configurable
 Bates Numbering: adds uniquely incremented identifiers with custom prefix, suffix, digit padding, and start number — used for legal discovery and archival
 

 Key accuracy rules:
 - Watermark tool adds a semi-transparent overlay — not security-grade or DRM-enforced
 - pdfFiller cannot remove cryptographically embedded watermarks — Erase tool covers visible watermark text visually only
 - Bates Numbering is a legal/archival-specific feature, not a general page stamp
 - Named competitors: ilovepdf, Smallpdf, Sejda, Adobe Acrobat, PDF24
